title: Quiz: Reliability Engineering for Quantum Systems

questions.question: What is a unique failure mode in quantum systems that does not exist in classical systems?
questions.explanation: Qubit decoherence (continuous degradation of quantum states) and calibration drift (gradual accuracy loss) are unique to quantum systems and require specialized reliability strategies.

questions.question: In an SLO framework, what is an 'error budget'?

options.text: The allowed amount of SLO violations within a period
The number of engineers assigned to errors

questions.explanation: An error budget represents the allowed amount of SLO violations within a time period. For example, 99.5% availability allows 0.5% downtime as the error budget.

questions.question: What are the three states of a circuit breaker?
questions.explanation: Circuit breakers have three states: Closed (normal operation), Open (failing, rejecting requests), and Half-Open (testing if the backend has recovered).

questions.question: When should a quantum-aware retry strategy NOT retry an error?

options.text: On hardware failures like qubit errors

questions.explanation: Hardware failures like qubit errors should not be retried as they indicate a fundamental problem with the quantum hardware that won't be resolved by retrying.

questions.question: What is the purpose of graceful degradation in quantum applications?

options.text: To progressively reduce functionality while maintaining core services
To disable all features at once

questions.explanation: Graceful degradation progressively reduces non-essential functionality when resources are constrained, while maintaining core services to provide the best possible user experience.

questions.question: What type of health probe should be used to determine if the application can handle incoming requests?
questions.explanation: Readiness probes determine if an application can handle requests by checking critical dependencies. Liveness probes only check if the process is alive.

questions.question: In exponential backoff with jitter, what is the purpose of adding jitter?

options.text: To prevent thundering herd when multiple clients retry simultaneously

questions.explanation: Jitter adds randomness to retry delays to prevent multiple clients from retrying at exactly the same time (thundering herd), which would overload the recovering service.

questions.question: A circuit fidelity SLO target of 85% means that 85% of circuits should execute successfully.
questions.explanation: A circuit fidelity SLO of 85% means the quantum circuit should achieve at least 85% fidelity (result quality), not that 85% of circuits succeed. Success rate would be measured by an availability SLO.
